last_item: Audit session auth — close the server-side logout invalidation gap
last_result: done — logout now rotates the HMAC session secret to invalidate all issued tokens, gated by logoutAuthorized (valid token + CSRF check for cookie flows) so the public endpoint can't be abused as an unauthenticated force-logout/DoS; store/auth/server tests cover each property; reviewer found no blocking issues (added Bearer-logout test it suggested); all gates green
notes: Session-auth audit item done (cookie hardening/expiry/rotation were already present+tested; added server-side logout invalidation). Next band-1 items: CSRF coverage proof on every state-changing endpoint (note: requireAuth already enforces a CSRF origin/header check for cookie unsafe methods — likely an audit+test iteration), then DB-level read-only role enforcement, secrets-at-rest (0600 + log-scrubbing), and login brute-force lockout (lockout exists in internal/auth — verify the throttle is end-to-end tested).
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
